originalTitle: Djibouti's president wins unprecedented sixth term with 97.8% of vote
title: Big News from Djibouti: A Leader Stays for More Time!
content:
Hello from Africa!

Hi everyone! It's your friendly news reporter, bringing you exciting stories from our amazing continent, Africa! Today, we have some really big news from a special country called Djibouti. It's about a very important leader there. Let's find out what happened!

Meet President Guelleh!

His name is Mr. Ismail Omar Guelleh. He is the main leader of Djibouti. Mr. Guelleh is 78 years old. That's a lot of birthdays! He helps make all the big decisions for everyone in Djibouti. It's a very, very important job!

What is a President?

Did you know that many countries, like Djibouti, have a leader called a President? A President is like the head of a very big family, but instead of just your house, it's for the whole country! They work hard to keep everyone safe and happy. They help make sure there are good roads, schools for kids, and clean water. It's a job where you have to think about many, many people every day. They are chosen to guide their country.

Where is Djibouti?

Let's find Djibouti on our big map of Africa! Djibouti is a small country in East Africa. It's right next to the big, blue sea. It's a sunny place, sometimes very hot! The people who live there are called Djiboutians. They are known for their warm welcome. Did you know Djibouti is famous for a super salty lake called Lake Assal? It's one of the saltiest lakes in the whole world! You can even float easily in it, like a boat!

What Does it Mean to "Win"?

In many countries, people get to choose their leader by voting. Voting is like picking your favourite colour or your favourite game, but it's for who will lead the country! Everyone who is old enough gets to make a special choice. The person who gets the most choices, or 'votes,' wins! Mr. Guelleh got almost all the choices this time. Like if 100 people voted, 97 of them chose him! That's a huge number!

President for a Long, Long Time!

Mr. Guelleh has been the President of Djibouti for a very, very long time. Can you guess how long? He has been President for 27 years already! That's more years than many of your parents have even been alive! And now, he will be President for more years. This is his sixth time winning to be President. Imagine being in the same class for six years in a row! That's how long he's been chosen to lead. Did you know that in some countries, a president can only be in charge for a few years, like two times? But in Djibouti, it can be for many more!

What About Other Leaders?

Sometimes, other people want to be President too. They are called 'candidates' or 'people who want to lead.' In Djibouti, some of these other people were not happy with how the choosing was done this time. They felt it wasn't fair. So, they decided not to join the big vote. This means that fewer choices were available for the people to pick from. It's a bit like if you were going to play a game, but some of the players decided not to come because they didn't like the rules. So only one team played!

Why is This Important?

When a leader stays for a very long time, it can mean different things for a country. Some people might really like it because they know the leader well. They feel safe and things stay the same. Other people might want a new leader. They might want new ideas and new ways of doing things for their country. They want different voices to be heard. This is important because the President makes big decisions that affect all the children and grown-ups in Djibouti. From building new schools to making sure there is enough food for everyone.

What Happens Next?

So, Mr. Guelleh will continue to be the President and lead Djibouti. He will keep working for the people of Djibouti, and everyone hopes for a bright and happy future for their country. That's all the news from Djibouti for now! Keep exploring and learning, my friends!
summary: Mr. Ismail Omar Guelleh, 78, has won again to be the President of Djibouti, meaning he will lead the country for even more years after already being in charge for 27 years, though some other leaders chose not to join the vote.
whyMatters: This is important because the President makes big choices for everyone in Djibouti, and when a leader stays for a very long time, it can make some people happy and others want new ideas for their country.

title: Nearly 400 sentenced in Nigeria for links to militant Islamists

originalTitle: UK shelves Chagos deal after Trump opposition
title: A Story About Islands, Friends, and Promises!
content:
Hello, hello, wonderful friends from our amazing continent of Africa! It's your friendly news reporter, always ready to share exciting stories from near and far. Today, we have a very important story about some special islands, a big promise, and why sometimes plans change.

What Are the Chagos Islands?

Imagine a place far, far away, in the big blue ocean. It's called the Indian Ocean, and it's HUGE! In this ocean, there are many tiny islands grouped together. These beautiful places are called the Chagos Islands. They are like little jewels floating on the water. They have white sandy beaches, sparkling clear water, and lots of happy fish swimming around. It sounds like a dream place, doesn't it? Did you know? The Chagos Islands are made from coral! Coral is like a special rock made by tiny sea animals. It grows slowly over many, many years to make islands and homes for fish.

A Long Time Ago: People Lived There

Many years ago, people lived on these beautiful Chagos Islands. They were called the Chagossians. They had their homes, their families, and their own special way of life there. It was their home, just like your home is special to you. But then, something big happened. A long time ago, people were told they had to leave their homes. They had to move away from the islands they loved so much. This made many people very sad. Why did they have to move? Because another country, America, wanted to build a very big airport there. This airport was for their big airplanes, used for important missions far away. So, the Chagossians had to leave. Did you know? Building an airport is a really big job! It needs lots of space for long runways so huge airplanes can land and take off safely. That's why they needed the islands.

Who Looks After the Islands Now?

After the Chagossians moved, the islands were looked after by a country called the United Kingdom, or the UK for short. The UK is a country very far away from Africa. But another country, Mauritius, which is a lovely island country near Africa, said: "No, these islands should be ours! They are close to us, and the people who lived there are like our family." So, there was a big question: Who should truly be the boss of the Chagos Islands? The UK said it was them. Mauritius said it should be them. It was a big discussion between these countries. Did you know? Mauritius is a beautiful country in Africa, with amazing beaches and friendly people! It's not too far from Madagascar.

The Big Idea: A Promise for the Islands

For many years, people talked and talked about the Chagos Islands. The Chagossians, who had to leave, always hoped they could go back home one day. Then, the UK government had a big idea. The UK leaders thought it would be fair to give the Chagos Islands to Mauritius. It was like making a big promise to give the islands back. This would have been a very happy day for Mauritius and for the Chagossians. It meant that the islands would be looked after by a country closer to them, and maybe, one day, the people could return. It was a plan to make things right, after so many years. Everyone was excited about this big promise.

A Powerful "No" from Far Away

But then, something unexpected happened. A very powerful person, Mr. Trump, who used to be the leader of America, said "No!" to this plan. Mr. Trump was worried about the big airport on one of the Chagos Islands. It's called Diego Garcia. He thought it was very important for America to keep using that airport for their big planes. So, because America wanted to keep the airport, Mr. Trump said he didn't want the UK to give the islands to Mauritius. His word was very powerful. Did you know? Leaders of countries have very important jobs! They make big decisions that can affect many people and places around the world.

What Does "Shelved" Mean?

Because of Mr. Trump saying "No," the UK had to change their plans. They said they have "shelved" the deal. This is a big, grown-up word. What does "shelved" mean? It's like when you have a book you want to read, but you don't have time right now. So, you put it on a shelf to read later. You don't throw it away; you just put it aside for a while. So, the plan to give the Chagos Islands to Mauritius is not happening right now. The UK government said they just "ran out of time" to finish the promise. This means the islands are still with the UK for now. The big promise is on hold, like a movie paused in the middle. Did you know? Countries often make agreements, which are like big promises or plans, with each other. It takes a lot of talking and planning to make them work.

What Happens Next?

So, what happens now? The Chagos Islands are still looked after by the UK. The big airport for America's planes is still there. And the Chagossians, who wish to go home, are still waiting. But the story is not over! Just because the plan is "shelved" doesn't mean it's forgotten forever. People will keep talking. Mauritius will keep asking. And maybe, one day, the UK will pick that book up from the shelf again and continue the story. We hope so!

Why This is Important for Us!

This story about the Chagos Islands teaches us a few things, even if it's far away. It shows us how important homes are to people. It teaches us about fairness and keeping promises. It also reminds us that leaders of countries make very big decisions. Sometimes, these decisions can make people happy, and sometimes they can make people wait a little longer for what they hope for. Let's always remember to be kind and fair to everyone, just like we hope countries will be kind and fair to each other. This is a lesson we can all learn, from our small villages to the big world! That's all for today's news, my bright young friends! Keep smiling, keep learning, and keep being awesome. See you next time!
summary: A plan by the UK to give the beautiful Chagos Islands to Mauritius was put on hold because a powerful American leader, Mr. Trump, didn't want to move a big airport there, leaving the islands with the UK for now.
whyMatters: This story matters because it's about fairness and a promise to give islands back to an African country, which got paused because of a big airport. It shows how big decisions can affect people's homes and hopes, even far away.

originalTitle: Union Berlin appoint first female boss in Europe's top men's leagues
title: Wow! A Super New Coach Making History!
content:
Hello, amazing friends! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you some super exciting news from a faraway place called Europe!

Meet Our Amazing Football Hero!

Guess what? Something really cool just happened in the world of football! A super smart and talented lady named Marie-Louise Eta just got a very important job. She is now helping to lead a big football team called Union Berlin. This team plays in Germany, which is a country far, far away from us. Did you know? Football is the most popular sport in the world! Lots and lots of people love to play and watch it! Marie-Louise is helping the team as an 'interim head coach'. That means she's leading the team for a little while, like when your teacher's away and a guest teacher comes to help. It's a really special job because she is the very first woman to lead a men's team in one of Europe's biggest football leagues! That means she's doing something no other woman has done before in these super important games. She is making history and showing everyone just how amazing girls and women can be!

What Does a Football Coach Do?

You might be wondering, 'What exactly does a football coach do?' Well, think of a coach like a captain of a ship, or a very good teacher for a sports team. They don't play the game themselves when it's happening, but they teach the players how to be the best. A coach helps the players learn new tricks, like how to kick the ball better or how to pass it to their friends. They make special plans for each game, like a secret map to win! They also help players work together, like a big, happy family on the field. It's a big job to make sure everyone is strong, happy, and knows what to do. The coach inspires the team and helps them believe in themselves. Without a good coach, even the best players might get lost! Did you know? Some coaches also teach kids how to play football, just like maybe you have a coach for your school team or local club!

Union Berlin: A Team of Friends!

Now, let's talk about the team Marie-Louise is helping – Union Berlin. This team is from a big city in Germany called Berlin. They are a very special team because their fans, the people who love them, are like a big family. The fans cheer very loudly and sing songs to support their players. It's like when your family or friends cheer for you when you're doing something great! Union Berlin plays against many other strong teams in Germany. They always try their best to win and make their fans proud. They work hard for each other, and that's why Marie-Louise is there to guide them. Did you know? Many years ago, the fans of Union Berlin even helped to build their own stadium, or football playground, with their own hands! How amazing is that?

Playing in the Big League: The Bundesliga!

Marie-Louise and Union Berlin play in something called the 'Bundesliga'. The Bundesliga is like a super big competition for football teams in Germany. Imagine all the best football teams in your country playing against each other to see who is the very best. That's what the Bundesliga is! It's one of the most exciting football competitions in the whole world. Teams play many games throughout the year, and they get points for winning or drawing a game. At the end, the team with the most points wins a big trophy, like a super shiny prize! It takes lots of skill and teamwork to be good in the Bundesliga. Did you know? Some of the fastest football players in the world play in the Bundesliga! They run super quickly with the ball!

Why This News is SO Special!

Now, let's get back to why Marie-Louise's new job is such big news. For a very, very long time, mostly men were the coaches for men's big football teams. But Marie-Louise has changed that! She is the *first* woman to ever get this kind of coaching job in one of Europe's top football leagues. This is like being the first person to climb a very tall mountain that no one else has climbed before! It shows everyone that girls and women are super strong, super smart, and can do anything they dream of. It tells every girl and boy watching that there are no 'boy jobs' or 'girl jobs' – only jobs for people who work hard and are good at what they do. Marie-Louise is a fantastic example for all of us, proving that if you have talent and work hard, you can reach the very top. She is opening doors for many others who will come after her. Did you know? In many parts of Africa, girls are also playing more and more football, showing their amazing skills and scoring lots of goals!

Marie-Louise's Journey: From Player to Coach!

Marie-Louise didn't just wake up one day and become a coach. She has loved football for a very long time! She used to be a super player herself. She played for big teams and even represented her country, Germany, in games. She knew all about how to play the game and work with a team. After she stopped playing, she wanted to help other players, so she started learning how to be a coach. She worked very hard, learning all the rules, all the strategies, and how to talk to players to help them improve. She studied a lot to become the best coach she could be. Before this big job, she was already helping Union Berlin as an assistant coach, which is like being a helper teacher. So, she knows the team very well, and the players know her and trust her. Her hard work and dedication have led her to this amazing new role. Did you know? Many of the best coaches in the world used to be great players themselves, just like Marie-Louise! They understand the game very deeply.

Dreams Can Come True for Everyone!

This story about Marie-Louise Eta is a wonderful reminder for all of us, especially you, my young friends. It shows that if you have a dream, you should work hard for it. No matter if you are a boy or a girl, if you are good at something and you try your very best, you can achieve amazing things. Maybe you dream of being a footballer, a doctor, a pilot, or even a news reporter like me! Marie-Louise's story tells us to believe in ourselves and never give up. She is breaking down walls and showing the world that talent and hard work are what truly matter, not whether you are a boy or a girl. She proves that everyone has the chance to shine brightly. Did you know? Your dreams are like seeds, and with hard work and belief, they can grow into big, beautiful trees! So keep watering your dreams!

A Big Cheer from Africa!

Here in Africa, we know all about amazing football! We have so many talented players and coaches, and we are super proud of them. Marie-Louise's story makes us all cheer even louder, because it's good news for everyone who loves football, everywhere in the world. It reminds us that from Accra to Cairo, from Nairobi to Lagos, girls and boys everywhere can look up and dream big. So, let's give a big clap and a cheer for Marie-Louise Eta! She is a true hero, showing us all the way to be brave and brilliant. Keep playing, keep learning, and keep dreaming, my wonderful friends! That's all for today's super news! See you next time for more amazing stories!
summary: A talented woman named Marie-Louise Eta is making history by becoming the first female coach for a men's football team in Europe's biggest leagues, showing everyone that dreams can come true for anyone who works hard.
whyMatters: This news is important because it shows all boys and girls that they can do any job they dream of, even if no one like them has done it before, proving that talent and hard work are what truly count.
